auth-fabric.subtitle: × Apex Logistics — Mid-Year Compliance Certificate Upload

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Naomi logs into Ridgeview's vendor portal.
scenes.lede: Naomi Bashir, Compliance Lead at Apex Logistics, enters her credentials on Ridgeview Procurement's vendor portal. Maestro intercepts and fires CLEAR at the login gate — before any sensitive action, identity verification is bound to the session.

beats.head: Naomi enters her vendor portal username and password.
beats.lede: The login lands on Ridgeview's vendor dashboard. Maestro detects the high-compliance risk and invokes CLEAR immediately — certification uploads are too sensitive to proceed without session-level identity proof.

beats.head: Maestro routes Naomi through CLEAR.
beats.lede: Tenant-branded CLEAR modal opens inline. Ridgeview's branding wraps the identity verification step — Naomi sees Ridgeview, not a generic third-party service.

beats.head: CLEAR session opens on Naomi's phone.
beats.lede: One tap. The CLEAR session opens on her device — no app download, no separate verification account. ID scan, selfie, biometric match run in parallel.

beats.head: ID verification and biometric match confirm Naomi's identity.
beats.lede: CLEAR captures the government-issued ID, runs the liveness check against the selfie, and confirms the biometric match. Result posts back to the portal in 90 seconds.

beats.head: Session opens with Naomi's identity bound. Witness event #1 logged.
beats.lede: The authenticated session now carries Naomi's verified identity and timestamp. Maestro logs the CLEAR event as Witness Event #1 — the first link in the chain of custody.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Naomi uploads renewed insurance certificates in the authenticated session.
scenes.lede: Inside the verified session, Naomi navigates to the compliance update section. Maestro serves a dynamic webform pre-filled with Apex's current certificate data — no re-entry of what's already on file.

beats.head: Naomi clicks "Update Compliance Certificates" in the portal.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes her verified session and unlocks the compliance update path. The webform is Maestro-rendered, adaptive to her prior certificate records.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills the webform with Apex's current certificate data.
beats.lede: Carrier name, policy number, and expiration dates from the prior year auto-populate. Naomi reviews and uploads the renewed COI and FedRAMP attestation — only new data required.

beats.head: Naomi selects the certificate files and uploads them.
beats.lede: Two files uploaded inline within the session. Maestro validates each upload's structure — issuer match, date range, document scope — before accepting.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens the uploaded certificates for policy anomalies.
beats.lede: Renewal period flagged as on-time; both certificates match the prior issuer; no scope reduction detected. All-clear signal posts in the form feedback — Naomi sees the validation result in real time.

beats.head: Naomi submits. Witness event #2 logged to the verified identity.
beats.lede: Submit fires bound to the CLEAR-verified identity from Scene 1. Maestro logs the submission as Witness Event #2 — tied to the same verified person, same session, same timestamp chain.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the continuous identity thread linking both events.
scenes.lede: Olivia Mendez, Ridgeview's Compliance Analyst, opens Agreement Desk to review Naomi's submission. Maestro has been binding the CLEAR event and the upload event the whole time — Agreement Desk surfaces it as one continuous identity thread.

beats.head: Olivia opens Agreement Desk to the vendor compliance section.
beats.lede: The dashboard shows Apex Logistics' update ticket. Agreement Desk opens to Naomi's session activity — two witnessed events linked in sequence.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the CLEAR verification event.
beats.lede: First entry: "Verified by CLEAR · Naomi Bashir · Government ID · Date / Time." The entry shows the biometric confirmation and the session-binding timestamp.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the in-session certificate submission.
beats.lede: Second entry: "Certificate Upload · Naomi Bashir · COI + FedRAMP · Ridgeview validated · Date / Time." The submission timestamp links directly to the CLEAR session ID.

beats.head: Iris confirms the pattern match — no anomalies detected.
beats.lede: Iris flags both events as low-risk: same person, same session, expected flow. No step-up or re-auth required. The update is audit-clean.

beats.head: Defensible record. Chain-of-custody complete.
beats.lede: Olivia confirms the update with confidence. Apex's compliance status stays active; the audit trail shows identity was verified, action was authenticated, both events are linked. No questions from compliance review needed.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Olivia opens the new-vendor onboarding webform.
scenes.lede: Olivia Mendez, Compliance Analyst at Ridgeview Procurement, opens the new-vendor onboarding workflow for a Class-A trucking vendor. Maestro fires the intake webform — Olivia fills in Apex's company data, required certifications, and DEI attestation scope.

beats.head: Olivia selects "New Vendor Onboarding" from the procurement dashboard.
beats.lede: The intake webform opens — adaptive fields for vendor classification, location, contract value tier, and certification scope. Olivia enters Apex's details: Class-A trucking, $4.2M annual contract value, requires COI + FedRAMP.

beats.head: Maestro routes Olivia to the W-9 and DEI attestation questions.
beats.lede: The form adapts based on vendor classification. Olivia selects the required documents — W-9, Certificate of Insurance, FedRAMP attestation, and diversity certification questionnaire.

beats.head: Olivia pre-fills from ServiceNow and completes the webform.
beats.lede: Vendor name, address, EIN, and contact authority pre-load from ServiceNow. Olivia confirms the classification scope, indicates Naomi Bashir as the authorized contact, and submits.

beats.head: Maestro recognizes the package shape and routes to packaging.
beats.lede: The intake data triggers Maestro's orchestration — four documents (W-9, onboarding agreement, COI request, DEI attestation) and three verification tasks (entity, diversity status, authorized signatory).

beats.head: Packaging phase begins. Next scene: Maestro assembles the documents.
beats.lede: Maestro has the data it needs. The onboarding packet is about to be assembled and routed to Naomi for signature.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro assembles the four-document onboarding packet.
scenes.lede: Behind the scenes, Maestro pulls the right document templates and pre-fills them with Apex's data from the intake form. The packaging phase is invisible to Olivia — she sees the next step when it's ready.

beats.head: Maestro retrieves Apex's entity verification data from the SOS filing.
beats.lede: Naomi Bashir confirmed as authorized signatory for Apex Logistics LLC. The entity verification returns in parallel with document generation.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the four onboarding document templates.
beats.lede: W-9, vendor onboarding agreement, COI request letter, and DEI attestation form — all pre-populated with Apex's classification, contract scope, and required certifications.

beats.head: Documents pre-fill from ServiceNow and intake data.
beats.lede: Vendor name, authorized contact, contract value, insurance scope, and diversity certification questions all pre-fill. Zero re-entry required from Naomi.

beats.head: Recipient routing is built from the intake classification.
beats.lede: Routing rule: primary recipient Naomi Bashir (compliance lead); copy to Olivia (analyst). COI upload attachment routing specified. All configured before the envelope is signed.

beats.head: Four documents ready. Envelope prepared.
beats.lede: Maestro has the package assembled, pre-filled, and routed. Moment of truth: the packet is ready to send to Naomi for signature and the intake-to-execution transition.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Naomi receives the onboarding packet and signs.
scenes.lede: Naomi receives the Ridgeview-branded email with the onboarding packet. Four documents, one ceremony. Identity verification runs at signing time — CLEAR confirms Naomi as the authorized signatory before the documents execute.

beats.head: Naomi receives the email with the onboarding packet.
beats.lede: Ridgeview-branded email from Olivia: "New Vendor Onboarding — Apex Logistics — Four Documents Ready." One link opens the envelope in Naomi's preferred channel (web or mobile).

beats.head: Naomi reviews the four documents in the signing ceremony.
beats.lede: W-9, vendor onboarding agreement, COI request, and DEI attestation — all visible in sequence. Fields are pre-filled; Naomi confirms and signs.

beats.head: CLEAR identity verification runs at signing.
beats.lede: Before the first signature is adopted, Docusign routes Naomi through CLEAR. ID, liveness, biometric match — confirms her as Apex's authorized signatory.

beats.head: Naomi signs all four documents in one ceremony.
beats.lede: One signature adopted and applied across W-9, onboarding agreement, COI request, and DEI attestation. Certification request email is queued to route to the insurance broker the moment the execution clears.

beats.head: Execution confirmed. Documents certified. Certification request fires.
beats.lede: All four documents signed and delivered to the file. COI request routes automatically to Apex's carrier; Ridgeview's compliance team receives the executed onboarding packet simultaneously. First onboarding step complete.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Naomi and Olivia continue in the vendor Workspace.
scenes.lede: Naomi receives the invite to the vendor Workspace — all documents, compliance status, and renewal reminders in one place. When certification renewal rolls around, the Workspace remembers everything.

beats.head: Naomi receives access to the vendor Workspace.
beats.lede: Ridgeview-branded email invites Naomi into Apex Logistics' vendor Workspace. Onboarding status, active certifications, contract summary, and renewal calendar visible from day one.

beats.head: Compliance status and tasks in one place.
beats.lede: Overview shows vendor classification, contract status, and certification expiration dates. Tasks shows the COI delivery deadline (30 days), FedRAMP annual renewal (12 months), and DEI attestation update cycle (annual).

beats.head: Iris tracks the vendor relationship in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows the onboarding completion milestone. Ask Iris answers questions about contract terms, certification requirements, and renewal procedures — grounded in the executed onboarding documents.

beats.head: COI delivery task open.
beats.lede: The task shows Apex's COI request status and the delivery deadline (30 days). Naomi uploads the certificate when received from the broker — same Workspace, no separate email or portal.

beats.head: Vendor relationship in motion.
beats.lede: Onboarding complete, four documents executed, Naomi and Olivia in the same Workspace. Ridgeview's compliance team has the complete vendor record; when renewal time arrives, the prior cycle's evidence flows forward automatically.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Marcus Lee — Pre-Day-1 I-9 Document Upload

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Marcus logs into Cascade's employee onboarding portal.
scenes.lede: Marcus Lee, newly hired Caseworker I at Cascade County HR, receives his portal credentials via email. He enters his username and password. Maestro intercepts at login — pre-Day-1 I-9 verification is identity-sensitive, so CLEAR fires immediately to bind Marcus's verified identity to the session.

beats.head: Marcus enters his onboarding portal login from his phone.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes him as a new hire (background check cleared, start date confirmed). Maestro flags this session as requiring identity verification before any employment documents advance — I-9 upload is too regulated to skip.

beats.head: Maestro invokes CLEAR at the portal login.
beats.lede: Cascade-branded CLEAR step-up modal appears. Marcus is asked to verify his identity — the documents he's about to upload must be bound to a verified person.

beats.head: CLEAR session opens on Marcus's phone.
beats.lede: One tap. The CLEAR modal transitions to Marcus's device. No app, no separate account — ID scan, selfie, biometric match all run on his phone in under two minutes.

beats.head: CLEAR verifies Marcus's identity and captures the I-9 document type.
beats.lede: ID scan confirms the document type (driver's license, passport, etc.) and returns the ID details. E-Verify check runs in parallel — confirms Marcus's work authorization status.

beats.head: Verified session opens. Marcus's identity bound. Witness event #1 logged.
beats.lede: CLEAR returns to the portal with Marcus's verified identity, document type, and work-auth status. Maestro logs the CLEAR verification as Witness Event #1 — the first link in the employment chain of custody.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Marcus uploads his I-9 documents in the authenticated session.
scenes.lede: Inside the verified session, Marcus navigates to the I-9 upload section. Maestro serves an adaptive webform pre-filled with the document type CLEAR just verified — List A (passport/DL) or List B+C combination.

beats.head: Marcus clicks "I-9 Documents" in the onboarding checklist.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes his verified session and serves the I-9 webform. The form is pre-populated with the document type CLEAR confirmed — Marcus only needs to confirm and upload the image.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills the webform with CLEAR's document-type result.
beats.lede: List A selected (driver's license from CLEAR). Marcus uploads the image of both sides of his ID. Maestro validates the image quality and document match against CLEAR's result.

beats.head: Marcus uploads the I-9 document image.
beats.lede: Two uploads: front and back of driver's license. Maestro validates the image quality, confirms the name/DOB match with CLEAR's result, and flags any concerns in real time.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for document authenticity and anomalies.
beats.lede: Document match: clean. Name, DOB, and document type align with CLEAR's verification. No security features flagged as suspicious. All-clear signal posts in the form — Marcus sees confirmation immediately.

beats.head: Marcus submits the I-9 documents. Witness event #2 logged to the verified identity.
beats.lede: Submit fires bound to Marcus's CLEAR-verified identity from Scene 1. Maestro logs the I-9 upload as Witness Event #2 — tied to the same verified person, same employment start, same identity chain.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the continuous identity thread linking verification and I-9 upload.
scenes.lede: Karina Yusuf, HR Business Partner at Cascade County, opens Agreement Desk to review Marcus's pre-Day-1 submission. Maestro has been binding the CLEAR verification event and the I-9 upload event the whole time — Agreement Desk surfaces it as one continuous identity thread.

beats.head: Karina opens Agreement Desk to Marcus Lee's onboarding file.
beats.lede: The dashboard shows Marcus's new-hire onboarding status. Agreement Desk opens to his session activity — two witnessed events linked in sequence.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the CLEAR identity verification event.
beats.lede: First entry: "Verified by CLEAR · Marcus Lee · Driver's License · E-Verify Cleared · Date / Time." The entry shows the biometric confirmation and the document type captured.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the I-9 document upload.
beats.lede: Second entry: "I-9 Upload · Marcus Lee · List A Document · Cascade HR validated · Date / Time." The upload timestamp links directly to the CLEAR session ID.

beats.head: Iris confirms the pattern match — no fraud signals detected.
beats.lede: Iris flags both events as low-risk: same person, expected flow, E-Verify cleared. No additional ID review required. Marcus's I-9 compliance is complete.

beats.head: Defensible record. Chain-of-custody complete.
beats.lede: Karina approves the I-9 submission with confidence. Marcus's pre-Day-1 task closes. The audit trail shows identity was verified, I-9 documents were validated, both events are linked. Payroll and benefits can process on Day 1 — no back-and-forth required.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Karina opens the new-hire onboarding webform.
scenes.lede: Karina Yusuf, HR Business Partner at Cascade County HR, opens the new-hire onboarding workflow for Marcus Lee. Maestro fires the intake webform — Karina enters Marcus's position classification, salary, start date, and required benefit elections.

beats.head: Karina selects "New Hire Onboarding" from the HR dashboard.
beats.lede: The intake webform opens — adaptive fields pre-populated from the job posting and background check data. Karina confirms: position Caseworker I, Family Services division, start date in 9 days.

beats.head: Maestro routes to the employment documents section.
beats.lede: The form adapts based on job classification. Karina enters salary, direct deposit bank details, and benefits elections (health plan, FSA, retirement contributions).

beats.head: Karina pre-fills from Workday and completes the webform.
beats.lede: Most fields auto-load from Workday HCM — Marcus's name, address, manager assignment, department code. Karina confirms the benefits elections and submits.

beats.head: Maestro recognizes the package scope and routes to document generation.
beats.lede: The intake data triggers Maestro's orchestration — six documents (offer letter, I-9, W-4, direct deposit, benefits enrollment, code of conduct) and three parallel tasks (IT provisioning, Facilities desk assignment, benefits carrier setup).

beats.head: Document generation phase begins. Next scene: Maestro assembles the packet.
beats.lede: Maestro has everything it needs. The complete onboarding packet is about to be generated, pre-filled, and routed to Marcus.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro assembles the six-document onboarding packet.
scenes.lede: Behind the scenes, Maestro pulls the right document templates and pre-fills them with Marcus's data from the intake form and Workday. The packaging phase is invisible to Karina — she sees readiness when the documents are prepared.

beats.head: Maestro generates the offer letter from the position and salary data.
beats.lede: Offer letter pre-populated: position title, salary, start date, supervisor name (from Workday), and benefits effective date. Ready for Marcus's review and signature.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the six document templates and pre-fills all.
beats.lede: I-9 (awaiting Marcus's document type confirmation via CLEAR), W-4 (pre-filled with benefits elections), direct deposit authorization, benefits enrollment forms, and code-of-conduct attestation — all pre-populated from Workday and intake data.

beats.head: IT and Facilities requests are triggered in parallel.
beats.lede: Maestro queues IT laptop provisioning, badge printing, and Facilities desk assignment. These run in parallel while Marcus is reviewing and signing — Day 1 is ready when he arrives.

beats.head: Recipient routing is built from the position classification.
beats.lede: Routing rule: primary recipient Marcus Lee; copy to Karina (analyst); secondary route to benefits carrier and IT provisioning queue. All configured before the envelope is signed.

beats.head: Six documents ready. Packet prepared.
beats.lede: Maestro has the complete package assembled, pre-filled, and routed. The packet is ready to send to Marcus for signature and the intake-to-execution transition.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Marcus receives the onboarding packet and signs.
scenes.lede: Marcus receives the Cascade-branded email with the complete onboarding packet. Six documents, one ceremony. Identity verification runs at signing time — CLEAR confirms Marcus's work authorization and I-9 document type before the documents execute.

beats.head: Marcus receives the email with the onboarding packet.
beats.lede: Cascade County-branded email from Karina: "New Hire Onboarding — Complete Package — Six Documents Ready." One link opens the envelope in Marcus's preferred channel (web or mobile).

beats.head: Marcus reviews the six documents in the signing ceremony.
beats.lede: Offer letter, I-9, W-4, direct deposit, benefits enrollment, and code of conduct — all visible in sequence. Fields are pre-filled from Workday; Marcus confirms and signs.

beats.head: CLEAR identity verification runs at signing.
beats.lede: Before the first signature is adopted, Docusign routes Marcus through CLEAR. ID, liveness, biometric match, and E-Verify confirmation — establishes his work authorization and captures the I-9 document type.

beats.head: Marcus signs all six documents in one ceremony.
beats.lede: One signature adopted and applied across offer, I-9, W-4, direct deposit, benefits, and code of conduct. IT, Facilities, and benefits enrollment routes fire the moment the execution clears.

beats.head: Execution confirmed. Documents certified. IT and Facilities provisioning launches.
beats.lede: All six documents signed and delivered to the file. IT provisioning, badge printing, desk assignment, and benefits enrollment all launch simultaneously. Marcus's Day-1 setup is underway before he receives the final execution email.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Marcus and Karina continue in the new-hire Workspace.
scenes.lede: Marcus receives the invite to the new-hire Workspace — onboarding milestones, Day-1 logistics, manager intro, benefits overview, and mandatory training in one place. When his first 90-day review opens, the Workspace holds his complete onboarding history.

beats.head: Marcus receives access to his new-hire Workspace.
beats.lede: Cascade-branded email invites Marcus into his new-hire Workspace. Day-1 logistics (building access, parking, equipment pickup), manager intro, benefits effective date, and mandatory training schedule visible before he walks in the door.

beats.head: Onboarding milestones and tasks in one shared place.
beats.lede: Overview shows the start-date checklist with status. Tasks shows benefits effective date (first paycheck), mandatory FERPA training deadline (due before first case assignment), and manager check-in schedule — Marcus and Karina see the same state.

beats.head: Iris tracks his onboarding journey in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows every document signed, every provisioning step completed. Ask Iris answers questions about benefits, training requirements, and tenure-based eligibility — grounded in the executed onboarding packet.

beats.head: Mandatory FERPA training task open.
beats.lede: The task drills into the FERPA training module — required for Family Services caseworkers before first case assignment. Self-paced completion from the Workspace, with completion status visible to Karina.

beats.head: New hire ready — relationship started.
beats.lede: Day 1 complete, all systems provisioned, benefits enrolled, training underway. Cascade's HR team has the full audit trail. Marcus has a job that worked from his first morning — no waiting on IT, no missing paperwork, no confusion about benefits or training dates.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Avalon Café — Annual Renewal and Outdoor Seating Amendment

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Mei logs into Northbrook's licensee portal.
scenes.lede: Mei Ortega, operator of Avalon Café, receives the annual renewal notice and logs into Northbrook Licensing Bureau's licensee portal. Maestro intercepts at login — license amendments are regulatory-sensitive, so CLEAR fires immediately to bind Mei's verified identity to the session before any modifications are allowed.

beats.head: Mei enters her licensee portal login from her phone.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes her as an active restaurant licensee. Maestro flags the session as requiring identity verification — amendment requests must be bound to the verified applicant, not just someone with her password.

beats.head: Maestro invokes CLEAR at the portal login.
beats.lede: Northbrook-branded CLEAR step-up modal appears. Mei is asked to verify her identity — any license amendments must be authenticated by the confirmed owner.

beats.head: CLEAR session opens on Mei's phone.
beats.lede: One tap. CLEAR transitions to Mei's device. ID scan, selfie, biometric match — she's verified in under two minutes while managing her café from the counter.

beats.head: CLEAR verifies Mei's identity and confirms business-entity ownership.
beats.lede: Government ID scanned and matched against the Secretary of State record for Avalon Café LLC. Confirmed: Mei is the sole member and authorized signatory.

beats.head: Verified session opens. Mei's identity bound. Witness event #1 logged.
beats.lede: CLEAR returns to the portal with confirmation of Mei's verified identity and owner status. Maestro logs the CLEAR verification as Witness Event #1 — the first link in the amendment chain of custody.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Mei submits the annual renewal and outdoor seating amendment in the authenticated session.
scenes.lede: Inside the verified session, Mei navigates to the annual renewal section. Maestro serves an adaptive webform pre-filled with Avalon Café's current license data — renewal confirmation + amendment options.

beats.head: Mei clicks "Annual License Renewal" in the portal.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes her verified session and serves the renewal webform. Current license data (address, capacity, operating hours, food service classification) is pre-filled for confirmation.

beats.head: Maestro presents renewal confirmation and amendment options.
beats.lede: Mei sees the current license: 38 seats, indoor service, weekday/weekend hours confirmed. Below: amendment option for outdoor seating (+8 seats, deck permit required).

beats.head: Mei selects the outdoor seating amendment and updates the renewal.
beats.lede: Mei selects "Add outdoor seating area" and specifies 8 additional seats on the deck (enclosed with retractable roof). Maestro validates the amendment against zoning rules (restaurant use confirmed) and flags the required fire-marshal and health-dept re-review.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens the amendment for zoning and code conflicts.
beats.lede: Outdoor seating amendment: no zoning conflicts, fire-marshal review required, health-dept re-inspection required. Deadline for amendments flagged: 30 days before next inspection period. All-clear signal posts.

beats.head: Mei submits the renewal and amendment. Witness event #2 logged to the verified identity.
beats.lede: Submit fires bound to Mei's CLEAR-verified identity from Scene 1. Maestro logs the renewal + amendment submission as Witness Event #2 — tied to the same verified owner, same license, same identity chain.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the continuous identity thread linking verification and amendment submission.
scenes.lede: Trevor Bowman, Licensing Specialist at Northbrook, opens Agreement Desk to review Mei's renewal and amendment. Maestro has been binding the CLEAR verification event and the amendment submission event the whole time — Agreement Desk surfaces it as one continuous identity thread.

beats.head: Trevor opens Agreement Desk to Avalon Café's license file.
beats.lede: The dashboard shows Avalon's annual renewal and amendment request. Agreement Desk opens to Mei's session activity — two witnessed events linked in sequence.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the CLEAR identity verification event.
beats.lede: First entry: "Verified by CLEAR · Mei Ortega · Government ID · SOS-Confirmed Owner · Date / Time." The entry shows the biometric confirmation and business-entity match.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the renewal and amendment submission.
beats.lede: Second entry: "License Renewal + Outdoor Seating Amendment · Mei Ortega · 38→46 seats · Northbrook validated · Date / Time." The submission timestamp links directly to the CLEAR session ID.

beats.head: Iris confirms the pattern match — no fraud signals detected.
beats.lede: Iris flags both events as low-risk: same verified owner, routine renewal, expected amendment flow. No red flags. Amendment and renewal are audit-clean.

beats.head: Defensible record. Chain-of-custody complete.
beats.lede: Trevor approves the renewal and amendment with confidence. Fire-marshal and health-dept re-review are scheduled. The audit trail shows identity was verified, amendment was submitted by the confirmed owner, both events are linked. No compliance question will hold up Avalon's renewed license.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE
scenes.head: Mei submits a restaurant license application via the applicant portal.
scenes.lede: Mei Ortega, owner of Avalon Café, submits her restaurant license application through Northbrook's adaptive self-service webform. Maestro routes her through the right questions based on her business type — restaurant, 38 seats, new location.

beats.head: Mei opens Northbrook's business-license portal and selects "Restaurant."
beats.lede: The adaptive webform recognizes her intent and routes her to the restaurant license path. Questions appear in sequence: business structure (LLC selected), food service type (full-service restaurant), capacity (38 seats), location and zoning.

beats.head: Maestro adapts the webform based on her classification.
beats.lede: Restaurant path confirmed. The form auto-branches to relevant fields: operating hours, outdoor seating (if applicable), food-handler certifications, planned opening date, ownership details.

beats.head: Mei completes the webform with business and property data.
beats.lede: Mei enters: Avalon Café LLC, 88 Riverbend Ave, 38 seats, weekday/weekend hours, full-service restaurant, opening date 12 weeks out. She uploads property photos and the zoning confirmation from the city planning department.

beats.head: Maestro summarizes the configuration and routes to Northbrook's queue.
beats.lede: Application summary auto-generated: Restaurant, new applicant, 38 seats, full-service, no outdoor seating planned (yet), opening in 12 weeks. Maestro fires health-dept clearance check, fire-marshal schedule, and food-handler card verification in parallel.

beats.head: Application intake complete. Next scene: Maestro packages the documents.
beats.lede: Maestro has Mei's intent and data. The application packet is about to be assembled, reviewed by the licensing team, and routed back to Mei for signature and fee payment.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro assembles the application and inspection coordination packet.
scenes.lede: Behind the scenes, Maestro pulls the application documents, validates the zoning and health-dept clearance, and pre-fills the inspection coordination forms. The packaging phase is invisible — Trevor (the licensing specialist) sees readiness when documents are prepared.

beats.head: Maestro validates Mei's property zoning for restaurant use.
beats.lede: Cross-check with zoning database: 88 Riverbend Ave confirmed for restaurant use. No restrictions on food-service classification. Health department lookup queued in parallel.

beats.head: Health and Fire departments coordinate their review schedules.
beats.lede: Maestro fires health-department and fire-marshal review requests simultaneously. Calendar hold placed for health inspection (Mar 22), fire inspection (Mar 24), ABC inspection (Mar 26) — contingent on application acceptance.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the three license documents and pre-fills with Mei's data.
beats.lede: License application, regulatory compliance attestation, and fee authorization — all pre-populated with property address, business structure, capacity, operating hours, and fee amount ($480).

beats.head: Application and inspection sequence are configured.
beats.lede: Recipient routing rule: primary Mei Ortega (applicant); copy to Trevor (analyst). Inspection scheduling is contingent on application approval. Fee payment required to confirm inspection dates.

beats.head: Three documents ready. Packet prepared.
beats.lede: Maestro has the application packet assembled, health/fire coordination confirmed, and inspection sequence ready. Moment to send to Mei for signature and fee payment.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Mei receives the application packet and signs.
scenes.lede: Mei receives the Northbrook-branded email with the license application packet. Three documents, one ceremony, inline payment. Identity verification runs at signing — CLEAR confirms Mei as the verified business owner before the application executes.

beats.head: Mei receives the email with the application packet.
beats.lede: Northbrook-branded email from Trevor: "Restaurant License Application — Avalon Café — Application + Attestation + Fee Authorization." One link opens the envelope in Mei's preferred channel (web or mobile).

beats.head: Mei reviews the three documents in the signing ceremony.
beats.lede: Application, regulatory compliance attestation, and fee authorization — all visible in sequence. Fields are pre-filled from her intake submission. Mei confirms the details and proceeds to sign.

beats.head: CLEAR identity verification runs at signing.
beats.lede: Before the first signature is adopted, Docusign routes Mei through CLEAR. ID, liveness, biometric match — confirms her as Avalon Café LLC's authorized signatory and sole owner.

beats.head: Mei signs all three documents and pays the $480 fee inline.
beats.lede: One signature adopted and applied across application, attestation, and fee authorization. The $480 fee processes in the same flow; receipt issues automatically. Inspection scheduling confirmation fires the moment payment clears.

beats.head: Execution confirmed. Payment processed. Inspections confirmed.
beats.lede: All three documents signed, fee paid, and delivered to the file. Health-department, fire-marshal, and ABC inspections are now confirmed for Mar 22, Mar 24, and Mar 26. Mei receives the inspection schedule in the same email as the execution confirmation.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Mei and Trevor continue in the license Workspace.
scenes.lede: Mei receives the invite to the license Workspace — inspection schedule, pre-inspection checklist, application status, and renewal calendar in one place. When her license renews next year, the Workspace remembers everything.

beats.head: Mei receives access to the license Workspace.
beats.lede: Northbrook-branded email invites Mei into the license Workspace. Inspection schedule (health Mar 22, fire Mar 24, ABC Mar 26), application status, fee receipt, and license-issuance timeline visible from day one.

beats.head: Inspection schedule and preparation tasks in one place.
beats.lede: Overview shows the three inspection dates and the application approval status. Tasks shows the health-inspector checklist (most common findings for first-time restaurants), fire-code compliance items, and ABC requirements — Mei sees the same state as Trevor.

beats.head: Iris tracks the application in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows application received, fee paid, inspections scheduled. Ask Iris answers questions about inspection requirements, common findings, compliance standards, and renewal procedures — grounded in the executed application.

beats.head: Health inspection prep task open.
beats.lede: The task drills into the health inspector's checklist and a self-assessment tool. Mei reviews food-storage, handwash, temperature-control requirements — and can prepare ahead. Inspection passes first time.

beats.head: License issued — relationship ongoing.
beats.lede: Inspections cleared, license issued with QR-verifiable authenticity, Mei and Northbrook in the same Workspace. When renewal time arrives 12 months out, the Workspace holds the prior application, inspection records, and compliance history. Renewal is a confirmation, not a fresh application.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Safe Streets Initiative — Quarterly Grant Drawdown Request

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Rosa logs into Harbor's grantee portal.
scenes.lede: Rosa Patel, Executive Director of Safe Streets Initiative, logs into Harbor Foundation's grantee portal to submit the Q1 drawdown request. Maestro intercepts at login — grant drawdowns are high-value and fraud-sensitive, so CLEAR fires immediately to bind Rosa's verified identity to the session before any financial action is allowed.

beats.head: Rosa enters her grantee portal login from her phone.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes her as an authorized grantee contact for Safe Streets Initiative's $120k grant. Maestro flags this session as requiring identity verification — grant drawdowns must be bound to the verified executive director, not just someone with her password.

beats.head: Maestro invokes CLEAR at the portal login.
beats.lede: Harbor-branded CLEAR step-up modal appears. Rosa is asked to verify her identity — drawdown requests must be authenticated by the confirmed authorized signatory on the grant agreement.

beats.head: CLEAR session opens on Rosa's phone.
beats.lede: One tap. CLEAR transitions to Rosa's device. ID scan, selfie, biometric match — she's verified in under two minutes from her desk.

beats.head: CLEAR verifies Rosa's identity and confirms her role as authorized signatory.
beats.lede: Government ID scanned and matched against the grant agreement's signatory record. Confirmed: Rosa is the Executive Director and authorized drawdown requestor.

beats.head: Verified session opens. Rosa's identity bound. Witness event #1 logged.
beats.lede: CLEAR returns to the portal with confirmation of Rosa's verified identity and signatory status. Maestro logs the CLEAR verification as Witness Event #1 — the first link in the drawdown chain of custody.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Rosa submits the Q1 drawdown request in the authenticated session.
scenes.lede: Inside the verified session, Rosa navigates to the drawdown section. Maestro serves an adaptive webform pre-filled with Safe Streets' grant agreement details — Q1 drawdown amount, budget line items, and expended-amount certification.

beats.head: Rosa clicks "Submit Drawdown Request" in the portal.
beats.lede: The portal recognizes her verified session and serves the drawdown webform. Current grant data (grant amount $120k, Q1 allocation $30k, year-to-date spend) is pre-filled for reference.

beats.head: Maestro presents the line-item attestation and expended-amount certification.
beats.lede: Rosa sees the budget detail: Personnel ($18k allocated), Program Services ($8k allocated), Administration ($4k allocated). Rosa enters Q1 actuals: Personnel $17.5k expended, Program Services $7.8k expended, Administration $3.9k expended. Drawdown request: $29.2k (within Q1 allocation).

beats.head: Rosa certifies the spend breakdown and planned Q2 expenses.
beats.lede: Rosa attests: "I certify that the Q1 expenses shown are accurate and aligned with the approved budget. Planned Q2 spend: Personnel $18k, Program Services $8k, Administration $4k." Maestro validates that total spend stays within annual $120k cap.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens the request for financial anomalies or policy violations.
beats.lede: Spend validation: Q1 actuals within budget, year-to-date pace normal for a Q1, no category overruns. All-clear signal posts. Drawdown request ($29.2k) and planned Q2 spend ($30k) flagged as normal.

beats.head: Rosa submits the Q1 drawdown request. Witness event #2 logged to the verified identity.
beats.lede: Submit fires bound to Rosa's CLEAR-verified identity from Scene 1. Maestro logs the drawdown request as Witness Event #2 — tied to the same verified executive director, same grant, same identity chain.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the continuous identity thread linking verification and drawdown submission.
scenes.lede: Asha Morgan, Program Officer at Harbor Foundation, opens Agreement Desk to review Rosa's Q1 drawdown request. Maestro has been binding the CLEAR verification event and the drawdown submission event the whole time — Agreement Desk surfaces it as one continuous identity thread.

beats.head: Asha opens Agreement Desk to Safe Streets Initiative's grant file.
beats.lede: The dashboard shows Safe Streets' Q1 drawdown request. Agreement Desk opens to Rosa's session activity — two witnessed events linked in sequence.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the CLEAR identity verification event.
beats.lede: First entry: "Verified by CLEAR · Rosa Patel · Government ID · Grant Signatory Confirmed · Date / Time." The entry shows the biometric confirmation and the grant agreement signatory match.

beats.head: Agreement Desk displays the drawdown request submission.
beats.lede: Second entry: Q1 Drawdown Request · Rosa Patel · $29.2k Requested · Budget Validated · Date / Time. The submission timestamp links directly to the CLEAR session ID.

beats.head: Iris confirms the pattern match — no fraud signals detected.
beats.lede: Iris flags both events as low-risk: same verified signatory, routine drawdown flow, spend within budget. No anomalies. Drawdown and certification are audit-clean.

beats.head: Defensible record. Chain-of-custody complete.
beats.lede: Asha approves the drawdown request with confidence. Harbor's finance team can process payment immediately. The audit trail shows identity was verified, financial commitment was certified by the confirmed authorized signatory, both events are linked — defensible against any funder audit or compliance review.

intake.subtitle: × Safe Streets Initiative — New Grant Configuration

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Asha opens the new-grant onboarding webform.
scenes.lede: Asha Morgan, Program Officer at Harbor Foundation, opens the new-grant onboarding workflow for Safe Streets Initiative. Maestro fires the intake webform — Asha enters the grantee organization, grant size, project scope, budget tier, and reporting cadence.

beats.head: Asha selects "New Grant Onboarding" from the grant-management dashboard.
beats.lede: The intake webform opens — adaptive fields for organization profile, grant type (general operating vs. project-specific), funding amount, grant period, and reporting requirements. Asha enters: Safe Streets Initiative, $120k general operating grant, 12-month period, quarterly reporting.

beats.head: Maestro routes to the organization profile and grant structure questions.
beats.lede: The form adapts based on grant type. Asha confirms: 501(c)(3) status (verified via IRS database), executive director (Rosa Patel), board composition (5 members), and program focus (community violence prevention).

beats.head: Asha enters budget tier, reporting cadence, and equity-rider scope.
beats.lede: Asha selects: Budget Tier 2 ($100-150k), quarterly reporting, requires equity statement in grant agreement. Maestro routes to MOU scope and disbursement schedule questions.

beats.head: Asha completes the intake webform and submits.
beats.lede: Asha specifies: grant agreement execution required, master grant agreement template (Harbor standard), statement of work (SOW) required, first disbursement 30 days post-execution. Asha submits the intake configuration.

beats.head: Maestro recognizes the package scope and routes to document generation.
beats.lede: The intake data triggers Maestro's orchestration — six documents (master grant agreement, SOW, budget detail, reporting requirements, indemnification clause, equity rider), three parallel verification tasks (501(c)(3) status, authorized signatory, board member check), and disbursement scheduling.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro assembles the complete grant-agreement packet.
scenes.lede: Behind the scenes, Maestro pulls the grant agreement templates, generates the SOW and budget detail from Asha's configuration, and pre-fills them with Safe Streets' data. The packaging phase is invisible — Asha sees readiness when documents are prepared.

beats.head: Maestro verifies Safe Streets' 501(c)(3) status via IRS lookup.
beats.lede: IRS database confirmation: Safe Streets Initiative, EIN 12-3456789, 501(c)(3) active, no debarment flags. Entity verification clean.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the six grant documents and pre-fills with grant data.
beats.lede: Master grant agreement (Harbor standard), statement of work (community violence-prevention programs, $120k allocation), budget detail (personnel $75k, program $35k, administration $10k), reporting requirements (quarterly financial + impact report), indemnification clause, and equity rider (requiring grantee to report on program access and outcomes by demographic).

beats.head: Maestro generates the disbursement schedule from the grant period.
beats.lede: 12-month grant period, quarterly disbursement: Q1 $30k (due 30 days post-execution), Q2-Q4 $30k each (due first of quarter). Drawdown process (attestation required) configured in the Workspace.

beats.head: Recipient routing is built from the organization profile.
beats.lede: Routing rule: primary recipient Rosa Patel (Executive Director); copy to Asha (program officer); secondary copy to Harbor finance (disbursement processing). All configured before the envelope is signed.

beats.head: Six documents ready. Grant packet prepared.
beats.lede: Maestro has the complete grant packet assembled, 501(c)(3) verified, budget detailed, and disbursement configured. The packet is ready to send to Rosa for signature and the intake-to-execution transition.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Rosa receives the grant packet and signs.
scenes.lede: Rosa receives the Harbor-branded email with the complete grant-agreement packet. Six documents, one ceremony. Identity verification runs at signing — CLEAR confirms Rosa as Safe Streets' authorized signatory and board member before the grant documents execute.

beats.head: Rosa receives the email with the grant agreement packet.
beats.lede: Harbor-branded email from Asha: "New Grant Agreement — Safe Streets Initiative — Master Agreement + SOW + Budget + Reporting Requirements + Equity Rider." One link opens the envelope in Rosa's preferred channel (web or mobile).

beats.head: Rosa reviews the six documents in the signing ceremony.
beats.lede: Master grant agreement, SOW, budget detail, reporting requirements, indemnification, and equity rider — all visible in sequence. Field definitions and dollar amounts are pre-filled from the grant configuration; Rosa reviews and confirms.

beats.head: CLEAR identity verification runs at signing.
beats.lede: Before the first signature is adopted, Docusign routes Rosa through CLEAR. ID, liveness, biometric match — confirms her as Safe Streets' authorized signatory and validates her board member status against the board resolution on file.

beats.head: Rosa signs all six documents in one ceremony.
beats.lede: One signature adopted and applied across master agreement, SOW, budget, reporting requirements, indemnification, and equity rider. Harbor finance is queued to release the Q1 disbursement ($30k) the moment all six documents clear execution.

beats.head: Execution confirmed. Documents certified. Q1 disbursement released.
beats.lede: All six documents signed and delivered to the file. Harbor's finance team receives the executed packet simultaneously with Asha. Q1 disbursement ($30k) processes within 24 hours. Rosa receives the payment confirmation and the Workspace access link in the same email as the execution confirmation.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Rosa, Asha, and Harbor staff continue in the grant Workspace.
scenes.lede: Rosa receives the invite to the grant Workspace — disbursement schedule, quarterly reporting tasks, impact-data milestones, and amendment workflow in one place. When the grant cycle renews, the Workspace holds the prior-cycle evidence and compliance history.

beats.head: Rosa receives access to the grant Workspace.
beats.lede: Harbor-branded email invites Rosa into the grant Workspace. Grant summary, disbursement schedule ($30k per quarter), reporting deadlines (quarterly financial + impact report), and equity-rider reporting requirements visible from day one.

beats.head: Grant status and tasks in one shared place.
beats.lede: Overview shows the grant summary (amount, period, program focus) and disbursement status (Q1 released, Q2-Q4 pending quarterly reports). Tasks shows the quarterly reporting deadline (Q1 report due by Apr 30), impact-data windows (program participation counts, demographic breakdowns, outcome metrics), and amendment process — Rosa and Asha see the same state.

beats.head: Iris tracks the grant in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows every disbursement completed, every quarterly report received. Ask Iris answers questions about reporting requirements, budget line-item tracking, and renewal timeline — grounded in the executed grant agreement.

beats.head: Q1 reporting task open.
beats.lede: The task shows the quarterly report template (financial summary, program participation counts by demographic, outcome metrics, any challenges or pivots), the due date (Apr 30), and the submission process (upload from the Workspace — no separate email or form).

beats.head: Grant active — impact in motion.
beats.lede: Q1 disbursement released, Safe Streets' work underway, Rosa and Harbor in the same Workspace. Harbor's compliance team has the complete grant record — signed agreement, budget, SOW, equity commitments, and every quarterly financial + impact report. When renewal time arrives, the prior-cycle evidence flows forward and the renewal cycle starts pre-filled, not from zero.
